Home.tagline: Software engineer. I architect systems, write about distributed computing, and care deeply about performance.

About.title: About
About.p1: I'm a backend engineer who builds real-time communication systems and developer tools. I specialize in high-throughput messaging, WebRTC video infrastructure, and integrations that bridge different communication protocols.
About.p2: I work at the intersection of Node.js, Meteor, MongoDB, and WebRTC — building features that serve government and enterprise clients at scale. I also maintain upstream open source forks, contribute back patches, and use AI/LLM tools to automate code review, generate tests, and build transcription pipelines.

myoffice.title: Middle+ Software Engineer — Backend Developer
myoffice.stack: Stack: Node.js, Meteor, MongoDB, WebRTC, Matrix, Docker
myoffice.desc: Developed Squadus — a Rocket.Chat-derived corporate messenger and digital workspace for the Russian market, serving government and enterprise clients.
bullets: Built real-time messaging backend (1:1/group chats, channels, threads) on Node.js/Meteor; optimized MongoDB aggregation pipelines and replica set sharding for high-throughput message history at scale.
Developed WebRTC-based video conferencing (VKS) backend — signaling server, SFU/media relay integration, and AI transcription/summarization pipeline for meeting recordings.

Architected Telegram integration via the Matrix protocol — first Russian corporate messenger with bidirectional Telegram bridging, enabling secure cross-platform communication while preserving DLP and encryption controls.
Designed ICAP-based DLP integration layer for InfoWatch Traffic Monitor and SearchInform CIB, enabling real-time message/file content inspection and policy enforcement.
Built scalable file/media service with chunked upload, thumbnail generation, PDF preview, and progressive image loading optimized for low-bandwidth mobile environments.

Improved application performance: reduced mobile cold start by 24% (Android) / 9% (iOS), halved Squadus 1.9 launch time, and stabilized WebRTC under mass concurrent load via connection pooling and MongoDB index optimization.
Maintained Rocket.Chat upstream fork — rebased 384+ upstream changes in v1.1, ported security patches, and contributed back Matrix federation components.
Managed infrastructure: Docker Compose / Kubernetes deployment, CI/CD pipelines, MongoDB replica set failover, Prometheus/Grafana monitoring.

epam.title: Junior Backend Developer
epam.stack: Stack: Node.js, AWS Lambda, DynamoDB, API Gateway
bullets: Developed Node.js microservices on AWS Lambda for an e-commerce platform serving the American Seeds Store (Syngenta).
Implemented authentication/authorization flows using JWT-based token validation across services.
Built error-handling middleware with structured logging, retry logic, and DynamoDB transaction error recovery.
Integrated DynamoDB for product catalog, order, and user data — designed single-table schema and optimized query patterns.
Contributed to CI/CD pipeline (AWS SAM / Serverless Framework) for automated deployments.
